title: excoecaria biglandulosa var. sellowiana
intro: Excoecaria biglandulosa var. sellowiana, also known as the poison tree, is a plant species native to the tropical forests of Central and South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including its ability to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions due to its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Excoecaria biglandulosa var. sellowiana include reducing inflammation, relieving pain, and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of this plant can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat various health conditions, although it is essential to note that its toxicity requires proper handling and preparation.
